preamble: The shift from the glottal fricative [h] to the velar stop [k] is a form of fortition (strengthening). It frequently occurs in historical clusters, loanword adaptations into fortis-heavy languages, or specific morphological environments like in Swiss German.

examples.note: Pre-sibilant fortition where the fricative hardened into a stop component.
Loanword hardening into the native fortis affricate system.

sources: Waterman: A History of the German Language
